1960s.title: Food security and planning-era price pressure
1960s.body: Droughts, food imports, administered prices, and the Green Revolution shaped India's 1960s price environment before the later liberalization era.
1960s.event: Food shortages, devaluation, and Green Revolution buildup
1960s.driver: Food supply, procurement prices, import dependence
1960s.outcome: Older rupee amounts compound across decades of structural change.
1960s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1970s.title: Oil shocks and administered prices
1970s.body: The oil shocks, state-administered prices, and food-market volatility made the 1970s a high-pressure decade for Indian household budgets.
1970s.event: Global oil shocks and domestic price controls
1970s.driver: Oil imports, food prices, fiscal pressure
1970s.outcome: Energy and food costs became central to purchasing-power loss.

1980s.title: Deficits before the reform break
1980s.body: Higher public spending and external borrowing supported growth but also set up macroeconomic strain before the 1991 balance-of-payments crisis.
1980s.driver: Fiscal deficits, import bills, credit expansion
1980s.outcome: The decade sits between planning-era controls and the reform economy.
1980s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1990s.title: Liberalization changes the inflation map
1990s.body: The 1991 reforms, exchange-rate adjustment, trade opening, and financial-sector changes altered how prices moved through the economy.
1990s.driver: Exchange rate, import liberalization, administered prices
1990s.outcome: Price formation became more market-linked as the economy opened.

2000s.title: High growth meets food and fuel stress
2000s.body: Rapid growth, credit expansion, and the 2008 commodity shock put food and fuel prices at the center of India's inflation debate.
2000s.event: Credit boom and 2008 global commodity shock
2000s.outcome: Purchasing power eroded even during years of strong headline growth.
2000s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

2010s.title: Inflation targeting and supply shocks
2010s.body: India moved toward formal inflation targeting while food shocks, GST implementation, and fuel prices kept household costs politically important.
2010s.driver: Food, fuel, tax transition, monetary policy
2010s.outcome: Policy credibility improved, but price shocks still reached consumers quickly.

2020s.title: Pandemic recovery and food inflation
2020s.body: Supply disruption, energy swings, and food-price pressure made the early 2020s a visible test of household purchasing power.
2020s.event: COVID-19 recovery and global commodity volatility
2020s.driver: Food, fuel, supply chains, interest rates
2020s.outcome: The 2024 comparison shows decades of compounding rather than a single-year shock.
